items.title: Why Anthropic's Mythos Is a Systemic Shift for Global Cybersecurity
items.author: www.govtech.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been highly active in April 2026 with several major announcements. Most notably, Anthropic revealed 'Project Glasswing' and its Claude Mythos Preview model — an advanced AI system capable of autonomously finding software vulnerabilities including zero-days across major operating systems and browsers. The model was deemed too dangerous for public release, with access restricted to select entities including Amazon, Apple, Microsoft, Google, and NVIDIA. This announcement has sparked s

items.title: Claude Mythos Preview: Anthropic's Most Powerful AI (93.9% SWE-bench) — NxCode
items.author: www.nxcode.io
items.snippet: Anthropic has been on an aggressive release cadence in early 2026. The biggest news as of April 2026 is Claude Mythos Preview, released on April 7, 2026 — Anthropic's most powerful model to date, achieving 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified and 97.6% on USAMO 2026. However, due to its exceptional and potentially dangerous cybersecurity capabilities (including autonomous zero-day exploit chaining across major OSes and browsers), Anthropic has explicitly stated it will not make Mythos generally available

items.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Learning curves (March 2026)
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has published several notable research papers in early 2026. On the economic front, they released a labor market impact study ('Labor market impacts of AI: A new measure and early evidence') presenting a new framework for measuring AI's effect on employment, along with two Anthropic Economic Index reports (January and March 2026) tracking Claude usage patterns and introducing 'economic primitives' as new building blocks for understanding AI deployment. items.title: Claude API Release Notes Overview
items.author: docs.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been active with API updates in early 2026. Major releases include Claude Managed Agents entering public beta — a fully managed agent harness for running Claude autonomously with secure sandboxing, built-in tools, and server-sent event streaming (accessed via the `managed-agents-2026-04-01` beta header). Alongside this, Anthropic launched the `ant` CLI, a command-line client for the Claude API that integrates natively with Claude Code and supports versioning of API resources in YAM
